title: Transactional Human-State Reference Implementation
description: Use the existing temporal human-state subsystem as the first complete consumer of journaled canonical mutation: integrate CLI, generated views, events, privacy classification, consent, redaction, expiration, revocation, recovery, receipts, audit reconstruction, and model-behavior evaluation.
acceptance_criteria: All human-state mutations and model-feedback publications use journaled, recoverable, receipted operations
Canonical collections, CLI commands, generated indexes, and the authority manifest are integrated
Privacy classification, consent, purpose limitation, redaction, expiration, and revocation are enforced
Recovery and forensic audit detect or resolve incomplete human-state operations
Model-behavior fixtures preserve context and uncertainty and reject superseded, retracted, or unauthorized state
